title: 怪屋
summary:
Accompanied by Josephine, Charles enters the house and heads to the kitchen, where he meets an elderly, stout nanny. The nanny provides him with tea and cookies and mentions that Josephine had been hiding and writing in a notebook. The nanny also notes that the master had insisted on keeping Josephine at home despite the wishes of Miss Edith. During their conversation, the nanny refers to the "old Leonidis" in the past tense.

Sophia joins them, expressing her terror and her belief that they are dealing with a "cold, calculating poisoner." When Charles asks who knew that the Iserlin eye drops could be lethal, Sophia explains that the method of murder was revealed by the old Leonidis himself during a family gathering. He had joked that if Brenda accidentally injected the eye drops as insulin, his heart condition would cause him to die. Charles concludes that the killer did not need a complex scheme because the victim had inadvertently provided the method. While Charles believes Brenda is unlikely to be the killer, Sophia remains skeptical.

The nanny dismisses the idea of a killer, suggesting instead that an intruder—possibly a Communist or a Catholic—might have entered through unlocked doors. Sophia then leads Charles to the living room for an early family meeting. She expresses suspicion toward Clementine, noting Clementine's fascination with the proletariat. In the living room, Charles finds the Leonidis family: Philip sits expressionless in a red velvet armchair, Roger appears disheveled and agitated by the fireplace, and Clementine sits nearby, avoiding eye contact.
